name: Adjacent category polished screen recorders
summary: Screen Studio ($9/mo annual positioning in reviews), OpenScreen (free OSS Screen Studio alternative), and Reframed (native Swift editor + capture) solve polished demos with auto-zoom, captions, and cursor choreography — not long-running timelapse. Users may compare TryLapse to these; positioning must stress zero-edit process video at tiny file size, not cinematic tutorial output.
key_findings: Screen Studio review: recording 5 min, editing 45 min problem; OpenScreen 37k+ stars, Electron, macOS 13+; Reframed 2026-native Swift, .frm projects, 120Hz cursor metadata; Web recorders cannot match cross-app cursor tracking per DEV analysis
implications_for_trylapse: Do not build auto-zoom timeline in v1; Optional export to MP4 for import into OpenScreen/Reframed; Marketing language: timelapse ≠ screen studio; Partner/integration story possible later
strengths: Adjacent tools validate screen-content monetization; Clear category boundary if messaging is disciplined
weaknesses: User confusion risk; Screen Studio brand dominates polished demo niche
metrics_or_signals: OpenScreen ~37.5k GitHub stars; Reframed 110 stars but rapid releases in 2026

primary_sources.title: Why web cannot beat Screen Studio
